This value does not account for the correct speed of the neutron, leading to an incorrect kinetic energy result.
This is incorrect due to a significant error in calculations, resulting in an order of magnitude mistake.

questions.explanation: The following is the solution:speed of neutron = distance travelled by neutron / time taken by neutron for covered that distance .speed of neutron = 24/3.6 × 10‐4 m/sspeed of neutron ( V)= 6.6 × 10⁴ m/sno ,kinetic energy = 1/2mv²K.E = 1/2 × 1.7 × 10-27 ×( 6.6 × 10⁴)² J= 1/2 × 1.7 × (6.6)² × 10-19 j=37.2 × 10-19 jso,K.E = 37.2 × 10-19.Hence the correct answer is A which is closest to the correct answer

questions.text: An automobile is moving forwards with uniform velocity due to the force exerted by its engine. If the force is doubled with the velocity remaining constant, what happens to its total power?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Doubling the force would result in an increase in power, but not by squaring it. Power is defined as the rate at which work is done or energy is transferred, and it depends on both force and velocity. If force is doubled while velocity remains constant, power would increase by a factor of 2, not squared.

Doubling the force while keeping velocity constant would result in an increase in power. If force is doubled, the work done per unit time (power) would also double, assuming no change in velocity. Therefore, the power would increase, not halve.

This is the correct option. Power is directly proportional to force when velocity is held constant. Therefore, if the force is doubled, the power exerted by the engine would also double. This is because power is defined as the product of force and velocity, and when velocity remains constant, any change in force directly affects power.

This option is incorrect. Doubling the force exerted by the engine, while keeping the velocity constant, would result in an increase in power, not a constant value. Power is directly influenced by force, so any change in force would lead to a corresponding change in power.

questions.explanation: Power = force * velocityWith velocity kept constant, Power is directly proportional to force. Therefore, doubling force would double power.

questions.text: You push a heavy crate down a ramp at a constant velocity. Only four forces act on the crate. Which force does the greatest magnitude of work on the crate?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: As the crate is moving at a constant speed, the net force is 0. The total of the forces exerted on the crate down the slope is equal to the forces up the slope.Forces down the slope = Pushing force + force of gravityForces up the slope = friction forceSince Forces up the slope = Forces down the slope ∴ friction = Pushing force + force of gravity Hence, friction is the greatest force.

The force of gravity acts down the ramp, helping the crate move. However, it is not the greatest force because it is part of the forces balanced by the force of friction, along with the pushing force.
The normal force acts perpendicular to the motion of the crate and thus does no work on the crate as work is done when force has a component in the direction of displacement.
The pushing force contributes to the motion of the crate downwards, but it is smaller than the force of friction because it is only part of the forces that friction is balancing.

questions.explanation: As the crate is moving at a constant speed, the net force is 0. The total of the forces exerted on the crate down the slope is equal to the forces up the slope.Forces down the slope = Pushing force + force of gravityForces up the slope = friction forceSince Forces up the slope = Forces down the slope ∴ friction = Pushing force + force of gravity Hence, friction is the greatest force.

questions.text: Power can be defined as the dot product of:
questions.subject: Physics

Power can be defined as the dot product of force and velocity, or:P = Fvwhere P is power, F is force, and v is velocity. The dot product is a mathematical operation that takes two vectors and returns a scalar value. In this case, the dot product of force and velocity is the scalar value of power.The power of an object is the rate at which it does work. Work is the product of force and distance, so power is the product of force, velocity, and distance.The dot product of force and velocity is a scalar value because it only takes into account the magnitude of the force and velocity vectors, and not their direction. The direction of power is the same as the direction of the force vector.

questions.text: A person "X" of 75 kg walks up a staircase in 15 s. Another person "Y" of mass 60 kg walks up the same staircase in 12s. The ratio of the power generated by "X" to that by "Y" is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The calculation for the power ratio does not yield this result.
This option is incorrect. This ratio does not match the calculated power ratio based on the given masses and times.
This option is incorrect. This ratio does not match the calculated power ratio based on the given masses and times.
The formula of power is:P = W/t = PE/t = mgh/tAs g and h is same for both X and Y, the ratio will be:Px/Py = mx/my × ty/txPx/Py = 75/60 × 12/15 = 1Hence, this is the correct option.

questions.explanation: To find the power generated by each person, we use the formula: P = mgh/t, where m is mass, g is gravitational acceleration (constant for both), h is height (same for both as they climb the same staircase), and t is time taken.For person X: Px = 75 * g * h / 15For person Y: Py = 60 * g * h / 12The ratio of their powers is given by: Px/Py = (75/60) * (12/15)Simplifying this gives: Px/Py = 1, meaning the power is equal for both, hence the correct answer is 1.Options A, B, and C are incorrect because they do not match the calculated ratio based on the given data.

questions.text: A field is said to be conservative if:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Work done inside that field is independent of the path followed
options.explanation: When a force is conservative, the work done in moving an object is independent of the path followed, and the work done in a closed path is zero. This principle is essential in various areas of physics, including mechanics and electromagnetism.

options.text: Work done in closed path inside that field is zero
options.explanation: Work done in closed path is zero because in closed path displacement is zero as it returns to its initial point at end.

i. The field in which work done on a body does not depend on the path followed by the body is called the conservative field.ii. The field in which work done in a closed path is zero is called the conservative field.
The field in which work done in a closed path is zero and work done is independent of path followed is called the conservative field.So, this option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: i. The field in which work done on a body does not depend on the path followed by the body is called conservative field.ii. The field in which work done in a closed path is zero is called conservative field.

questions.explanation: Option D is correct. When velocity is doubled, the momentum, which is calculated using p = m.v, increases by a factor of 2. The kinetic energy, calculated using K.E = 1/2 m.v2, increases by a factor of 4 because the velocity is squared in the formula. Option A is incorrect because it reverses the effects on momentum and kinetic energy. Option B is incorrect because both physical quantities do change with velocity. Option C misunderstands the relationship between velocity and kinetic energy, which increases fourfold rather than remaining constant.

questions.text: A moving body need not have:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Velocity is a vector quantity that describes the rate and direction of motion of an object. It is a measure of how fast an object is moving in a particular direction.

Momentum is the product of an object's mass and its velocity. It is also a vector quantity and represents the quantity of motion possessed by an object. In the absence of external forces, the momentum of a moving body remains constant (conservation of momentum).

This is the correct answer. A moving body need not have potential energy unless it is located at a certain height or position in a force field where potential energy is associated with its configuration or position.
Kinetic energy is the energy possessed by an object due to its motion. It is dependent on the mass and speed of the object. The kinetic energy of a moving body is determined by its velocity and mass.

questions.explanation: A moving body need not have "c) Potential energy."A moving body may have velocity and momentum due to its motion, but it does not necessarily have potential energy unless it is in a position or configuration where potential energy is relevant (e.g., elevated above the ground). The kinetic energy, on the other hand, is always associated with the motion of the body.

questions.text: What is the average power required to lift a mass of 100kg to a height of 50m in 50 Seconds?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The average power required to lift a 100 kg mass to a height of 50 m in 50 seconds is not 100 W.

To calculate the average power required to lift a mass, we can use the formula: Average Power = Work / Time The work done to lift a mass to a certain height is given by the formula: Work = Force x Distance In this case, the force required to lift the mass is equal to its weight, which is calculated as Force = Mass x Acceleration due to gravity Acceleration due to gravity is approximately 9.8 m/s². Now, let's calculate the work done: Work = (Mass x Acceleration due to gravity) x Distance = (100 kg) x (9.8 m/s²) x (50 m) = 49,000 J The average power can be calculated by dividing the work by the time: Average Power = Work / Time = 49,000 J / 50 s = 980 W
This option is incorrect. The average power required to lift a 100 kg mass to a height of 50 m in 50 seconds is not 50 W.
This option is incorrect. The average power required to lift a 100 kg mass to a height of 50 m in 50 seconds is not 5000 W.

questions.explanation: To calculate the average power required to lift a mass, we can use the formula: Average Power = Work / Time The work done to lift a mass to a certain height is given by the formula: Work = Force x Distance In this case, the force required to lift the mass is equal to its weight, which is calculated as Force = Mass x Acceleration due to gravity Acceleration due to gravity is approximately 9.8 m/s². Now, let's calculate the work done: Work = (Mass x Acceleration due to gravity) x Distance = (100 kg) x (9.8 m/s²) x (50 m) = 49,000 J The average power can be calculated by dividing the work by the time: Average Power = Work / Time = 49,000 J / 50 s = 980 W

questions.text: Protons, electrons, neutrons, and particles have the same momentum. Which of them has the highest K.E?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Protons have a larger mass than electrons, neutrons, and alpha particles. Therefore, protons have higher kinetic energy than electrons but lower kinetic energy than alpha particles.

The kinetic energy (K.E.) of an object is given by the equation: K.E. = (1/2) x m x v2 where m is the mass and v is the velocity of the object. Given that momentum is the same for all three particles, and momentum is given by the product of mass and velocity, we can conclude that the velocities of protons, electrons, and neutrons must be different. Since kinetic energy depends on the mass and the square of the velocity, the particle with the highest kinetic energy will be the one with the highest velocity. Therefore, the electron, which has the smallest mass among the three particles, will have the highest kinetic energy. The higher velocity of the electron compensates for its smaller mass, resulting in greater kinetic energy compared to protons and neutrons.
Neutrons have a mass similar to protons but are not as heavy as alpha particles. Hence, neutrons have higher kinetic energy than electrons but lower kinetic energy than alpha particles.
Alpha particles consist of two protons and two neutrons, giving them the highest mass among the given options.

questions.explanation: The momentum of a particle is given by the product of its mass and velocity. Since both protons and electrons have the same momentum, it means they must have different velocities due to their different masses.The kinetic energy (K.E) of a particle is given by the equation K.E = (1/2) mv^2, where m is the mass of the particle and v is its velocity.Comparing protons, electrons, and neutrons:Protons have a relatively larger mass compared to electrons and neutrons.Neutrons have a slightly larger mass than protons.Electrons have a much smaller mass compared to protons and neutrons.Given that momentum is the same for all three particles, and momentum is given by the product of mass and velocity, we can conclude that the velocities of protons, electrons, and neutrons must be different.Since kinetic energy depends on the mass and the square of the velocity, the particle with the highest kinetic energy will be the one with the highest velocity.Therefore, the electron, which has the smallest mass among the three particles, will have the highest kinetic energy. The higher velocity of the electron compensates for its smaller mass, resulting in a greater kinetic energy compared to protons and neutrons.

questions.text: For what angle between F and d work reduces to half of its maximum value?
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: If θ is 60 degrees, cos(60°) = 0.5. So, it reduces the work to half its maximum value.
If θ is 30 degrees, cos(30°) = √3/2 ≈ 0.866. This angle results in less than half of the maximum work.
If θ is 45 degrees, cos(45°) = 1/√2 ≈ 0.707. This angle also results in less than half of the maximum work.
If θ is 90 degrees, cos(90°) = 0. This angle results in no work being done, so it's not what we're looking for.

questions.explanation: W=FxdcosθW=Fxdcos(60°)W=1/2 Fxd

questions.text: Slope of energy-time graph is equal to:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: The slope of an energy-time graph is not equal to acceleration. Acceleration is the rate of change of velocity with respect to time and is unrelated to energy.
The slope of an energy-time graph is also not equal to momentum. Momentum is the product of an object's mass and its velocity and is not directly related to the energy-time relationship.
Power is the rate at which energy is transferred or converted per unit of time. The slope of an energy-time graph can indeed represent power, as it indicates how fast energy is changing with time.

Work is the energy transferred to or from an object when a force is applied over a distance. The slope of an energy-time graph is not directly equal to work but can indirectly relate to it if the energy change over time is due to work being done.

questions.explanation: Slope = Δ Energy/Δtime = Power

questions.text: You accidentally bump into a bookcase. It tilts briefly but then returns to upright, and you breath a sigh of relief. When you first bumped the bookcase, its center of gravity moved:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Upward and its gravitational potential energy increased.
options.explanation: Upward and its gravitational potential energy increased. The bookcase is in a stable equilibrium, as evidenced by its tendency to return to that equilibrium after being bumped. To obtain this stable equilibrium, the bookcase's potential energy must increase as it tilts so that it accelerates in the direction that reduces its potential energy as quickly as possible--namely back toward equilibrium. That arrangement requires that its center of gravity to rise, since gravitational potential energy is the only form of potential energy in this situation.

options.text: Downward and its gravitational potential energy decreased.
options.explanation: Incorrect. If the center of gravity moved downward, the bookcase would be in an unstable equilibrium, and it would not return to its original position. The gravitational potential energy would decrease, contrary to what is observed.

options.text: Downward and its gravitational potential energy increased.
options.explanation: Incorrect. A downward movement of the center of gravity would indicate a decrease in potential energy, not an increase, thus not supporting the stable equilibrium observed.

options.text: Upward and its gravitational potential energy decreased.
options.explanation: Incorrect. An upward movement of the center of gravity would lead to an increase in gravitational potential energy, not a decrease. This is consistent with the stable equilibrium described.
Incorrect. Option A correctly describes the movement and energy change of the bookcase's center of gravity.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is that the center of gravity moved upward and its gravitational potential energy increased. This increase in potential energy indicates that the bookcase is in a stable equilibrium, as it returns to its upright position after being displaced. This is consistent with the characteristics of stable equilibrium, where a displacement leads to an increase in potential energy, prompting the system to revert to its equilibrium state. The other options are incorrect as they do not align with the principles of stable equilibrium and the observed behavior of the bookcase.

questions.text: Two equal masses are attached to the two ends of a spring of spring constant K. They are then pulled out symmetrically so that the spring extends by x. The work done by spring force on each block is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Option A is incorrect according to the calculations.
Option B is incorrect according to the calculations.
Option C is incorrect according to the calculations.
Option D is correct as work done by the spring force = -1/2 kx² This work is done on both the blocks. Therefore, the work done on each block = -1/4 kx².

questions.explanation: Option D is correct as work done by the spring force = -1/2 kx² This work is done on both the blocks. Therefore, the work done on each block = -1/4 kx².

questions.text: By burning 1 g of coal, the energy produced is 2 kcal. Then for 1 kWh, the quantity of coal required will be nearly:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: This option is incorrect because it greatly overestimates the amount of coal needed based on the energy produced from 1 g of coal.
This option is also incorrect as it does not align with the conversion of energy produced per gram of coal and the energy amount needed.
This is the correct option, as it accurately reflects the calculations based on the energy produced by burning coal and the required energy in kWh.
This option is incorrect as it underestimates the amount of coal needed based on the given energy production rate.

questions.explanation: To find out how much coal is needed for 1 kWh, we start by converting kWh to kcal. Since 1 kWh is equivalent to 860 kcal, we can calculate the amount of coal needed. Given that burning 1 g of coal yields 2 kcal, we can compute:Energy from 1 g of coal: 2 kcalEnergy needed: 860 kcalCoal needed = (860 kcal) / (2 kcal/g) = 430 g = 0.43 kg.Since the question asks for the nearest option in kilograms, 0.43 kg is approximately equal to 2/5 kg. Therefore, Option C is correct.Options A, B, and D are incorrect because they either overestimate or underestimate the required amount of coal based on the data provided in the question.

questions.text: A block hangs freely from the end of a spring. A boy then slowly pushes the block upwards so that the spring becomes strain free. The gain in gravitational potential energy of the block during this process is equal to:
questions.subject: Physics

options.text: Work done by the boy against the gravitational force acting on the block
options.explanation: Option A is incorrect according to calculations.

options.text: Loss of energy stored in the spring minus the work done by the tension in the spring
options.explanation: Option B is incorrect according to calculations.

options.text: Work done on the block by the boy plus the loss of energy stored in the spring
options.explanation: Let xo be the initial extension of the spring. Then the initial energy stored in the spring is: ½kxo2The gain in gravitational potential energy: mgh = mgxoThe work done increases the gravitational potential energy and decreases the spring’s potential energy.The total energy gained = gain in the gravitational potential energy - loss in spring’s potential energy.Therefore, work done by the boy (wb) = gain in the gravitational potential energy - loss in spring’s potentialFrom here you can see that the gain in the gravitational potential energy of the block = work done by the boy + loss in the spring’s potential energy.Therefore, option C is the correct one.

options.text: Work done on the block by the boy minus the work done by the tension in the spring
options.explanation: Option D is incorrect according to calculations.

questions.explanation: Let xo be the initial extension of the spring. Then the initial energy stored in the spring is: ½kxo2The gain in gravitational potential energy: mgh = mgxoThe work done increases the gravitational potential energy and decreases the spring’s potential energy. The total energy gained = gain in the gravitational potential energy - loss in spring’s potential energy. Therefore, work done by the boy (wb) = gain in the gravitational potential energy - loss in spring’s potential From here you can see that the gain in the gravitational potential energy of the block = work done by the boy + loss in the spring’s potential energy. Therefore, option C is the correct one.

questions.text: A force F→= 8iˆ – 6jˆ – 10kˆ Newton produces an acceleration of 1ms–2 in a body. The mass of the body is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Option A is not correct according to calculations.

Understanding the ProblemWe are given a force vector and the acceleration it produces.We need to find the mass of the body.Newton's second law states that F = ma, where F is the force, m is the mass, and a is the acceleration.Steps to SolveFind the magnitude of the force:The force is given as F→ = 8iˆ - 6jˆ - 10kˆ Newton.The magnitude of the force (F) is calculated as:F = √(8² + (-6)² + (-10)²)F = √(64 + 36 + 100)F = √200F = 10√2 Newtons.Apply Newton's second law:F = maWe are given that the acceleration (a) is 1 m/s².Therefore, m = F / am = (10√2) / 1m = 10√2 kg.
Option C is not correct according to calculations.
Option D is not correct according to calculations.

questions.explanation: Understanding the ProblemWe are given a force vector and the acceleration it produces.We need to find the mass of the body.Newton's second law states that F = ma, where F is the force, m is the mass, and a is the acceleration.Steps to SolveFind the magnitude of the force:The force is given as F→ = 8iˆ - 6jˆ - 10kˆ Newton.The magnitude of the force (F) is calculated as:F = √(8² + (-6)² + (-10)²)F = √(64 + 36 + 100)F = √200F = 10√2 Newtons.Apply Newton's second law:F = maWe are given that the acceleration (a) is 1 m/s².Therefore, m = F / am = (10√2) / 1m = 10√2 kg.

questions.text: Choose the correct one.
questions.subject: English

options.text: The Taj Mahal was commissioned by Shah Jahan in 1631, to be built in the memory of his wife Mumtaz Mahal, a Persian princess.
options.explanation: The sentence is structured correctly, using the definite article "The" before the monument's name, and the preposition "in" before the year.

options.text: Taj Mahal was commissioned by Shah Jahan in 1631, to be built in memory of his wife Mumtaz Mahal, a Persian princess.

options.text: The Taj Mahal was commissioned by Shah Jahan in the 1631, to be built in memory of his wife Mumtaz Mahal, the Persian princess.

options.text: Taj Mahal was commissioned by Shah Jahan in 1631, to be built in the memory of his wife Mumtaz Mahal, the Persian princess.
options.explanation: The sentence is missing the definite article "The" before the monument's name, and uses the definite article "the" before "Persian princess" unnecessarily.

questions.explanation: The sentence is structured correctly, using the definite article "The" before the monument's name, and the preposition "in" before the year.

questions.text: In each question in the following, four alternative sentences are given. Choose the CORRECT one and fill the circle corresponding to that letter in the answer sheet.
questions.subject: English

options.text: Jan asked; “What did Joe mean when he said, ‘I will see you later.’”
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The semicolon after "asked" is not appropriate here. A comma should be used to introduce the quotation.

options.text: Jan asked, “What did Joe mean when he said, ‘I will see you later?’”
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The question mark is placed inside the single quotation marks, which is incorrect because "I will see you later" is not a question. The question mark should be placed outside the single quotation marks but inside the double quotation marks.

options.text: Jan asked, “What did Joe mean when he said, ‘I will see you later’?”
options.explanation: This option is correct. It places the question mark correctly inside the double quotation marks, indicating that the entire sentence is a question, but outside the single quotation marks since "I will see you later" is not a question.Correct Answer: C) Jan asked, “What did Joe mean when he said, ‘I will see you later’?”Option C is the correct choice. It places the question mark correctly inside the double quotation marks, indicating that the entire sentence is a question, but outside the single quotation marks because "I will see you later" is not a question

options.text: Jan asked, “What did Joe mean when he said, ‘I will see you later’”?
options.explanation: This option is incorrect. The question mark is placed outside the double quotation marks, which is incorrect. The question mark should be placed inside the double quotation marks because the entire sentence is a question.

questions.explanation: It places the question mark correctly inside the double quotation marks, indicating that the entire sentence is a question, but outside the single quotation marks since "I will see you later" is not a question. Correct Answer: C) Jan asked, “What did Joe mean when he said, ‘I will see you later’?” Option C is the correct choice. It places the question mark correctly inside the double quotation marks, indicating that the entire sentence is a question, but outside the single quotation marks because "I will see you later" is not a question

options.explanation: The word that best fits in the blank is "ecstatic."So the complete sentence is:"As soon as the home team scored, the fans became ecstatic in their excitement."Ecstatic means overwhelmingly happy or excited, which describes the fans’ reaction to the home team scoring.
This option is incorrect.
This option is incorrect.
This option is incorrect.

questions.text: Choose the correct option
questions.subject: English

options.text: The James Dillingham Young was very proud of two things which they owned.
options.explanation: The James Dillingham Young was very proud of two things which they owned. This option is incorrect because "was" is singular, but the subject "The James Dillingham Young" suggests a plural meaning. It should be "were" to match the plural subject.

options.text: The James Dillingham Youngs was very proud of two things which theyowned
options.explanation: The James Dillingham Youngs was very proud of two things which they owned. This option is incorrect because "was" is singular, but "Youngs" is plural. It should be "were" to match the plural subject.

options.text: The James Dillingham Youngs were very proud of two things which he owned.
options.explanation: The James Dillingham Youngs were very proud of two things which he owned. This option is incorrect because "he" is singular, but the subject "The James Dillingham Youngs" is plural. It should be "they" to match the plural subject.

options.text: The James Dillingham Youngs were very proud of two things which they owned.
options.explanation: The James Dillingham Youngs were very proud of two things which they owned.This option is correct. It uses the plural verb "were" to agree with the plural subject "The James Dillingham Youngs," and "they" correctly refers to the plural subject.Therefore, option D is the correct choice. It accurately describes how the James Dillingham Youngs (likely a family) were proud of two things they owned, using correct subject-verb agreement and pronoun usage.

questions.explanation: The James Dillingham Youngs were very proud of two things which they owned.This option is correct. It uses the plural verb "were" to agree with the plural subject "The James Dillingham Youngs," and "they" correctly refers to the plural subject.Therefore, option D is the correct choice. It accurately describes how the James Dillingham Youngs (likely a family) were proud of two things they owned, g correct subject-verb agreement and pronoun usage.

questions.text: Choose the correct option.
questions.subject: English

options.text: Within hours, realizing that one may have gone too far, the victim handed himself in.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because it uses "one" instead of "he," which changes the intended meaning and does not fit grammatically.

options.text: Within hours, realizing that he may have gone too far, the victim handed him in.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because "him" should be replaced with "himself" to indicate that the victim took the action on their own accord.

options.text: Within hours, realizing that he may have gone too far, the victim handed himself in.
options.explanation: Option (C) is the correct choice as it uses the reflexive pronoun "himself" appropriately to describe the victim's action of surrendering to the authorities.

options.text: Within hours, realizing that he may have gone too far, the victim handed oneself in.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect because "oneself" is a reflexive pronoun that should only be used when the subject and object of the verb are the same person. Here, "oneself" does not correctly reflect the victim's action of surrendering.

questions.explanation: Option (C) is the correct choice as it uses the reflexive pronoun "himself" appropriately to describe the victim's action of surrendering to the authorities.

questions.text: Two positions of a cube are shown below. What will come opposite to face containing 'δ'?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Face ∞ is adjacent to δ in the first cube, so it cannot be opposite to δ.
Face θ is not directly opposite to δ. It is adjacent to α in the second cube view.
Face β is opposite to δ because it is adjacent to α and not visible in the first cube view. Thus, it is not adjacent to δ in any view.
Face α is common in both views and cannot be opposite to δ.

questions.explanation: Face β is opposite to δ because it is adjacent to α and not visible in the first cube view. Thus, it is not adjacent to δ in any view.

questions.text: In the following question, two statements numbered I and II are given. There may be cause and effect relationship between the two statements. These two statements may be the effect of the same cause or independent causes. These statements may be independent causes without saving any relationship. Read both the statements in question and mark your answer as:A. If statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect;B. If statement II is the cause and statement, I is its effect;C. If both the statements I and II are independent causes;D. If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes;E. If both the statements I and II are effects of some common causes;I. Stefan's English-speaking power is very less.II. Stefan has decided to join Saint Pants English coaching class.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: A. If statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect;
options.explanation: Stefan's limited English-speaking skills could logically lead him to join an English coaching class to improve his abilities, making statement I the cause and statement II the effect.

options.text: B. If statement II is the cause and statement I is its effect;
options.explanation: Joining an English coaching class (statement II) typically occurs because of the need to improve English-speaking skills, not the other way around. Therefore, statement II cannot be the cause of statement I.

options.text: C. If both the statements I and II are independent causes;
options.explanation: There is a logical relationship between the two statements; Stefan's decision to join a class is not independent of his English-speaking skills.

options.text: D. If both the statements I and II are effects of independent causes;
options.explanation: Stefan's decision to join a class (statement II) directly stems from his need to improve his English-speaking skills (statement I), so they are not effects of independent causes.

options.text: E. If both the statements I and II are effects of some common causes;
options.explanation: While it might seem possible that a common cause like a job requirement could lead to both statements, the direct relationship suggests a clearer cause-effect link between statements I and II.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is option A. Stefan's limited English-speaking skills (statement I) logically lead him to join an English coaching class (statement II) to improve his proficiency. Therefore, statement I is the cause and statement II is its effect. Option B is incorrect because joining a class does not cause one's English-speaking skills to be low. Option C is incorrect as the statements are not independent; they are related. Option D is incorrect because the statements are not effects of independent causes; they are directly related. Finally, option E is incorrect because although a common cause could be possible, the direct link between the two statements is more evident.

questions.text: In this question, a statement is given followed by two courses of action numbered I and II. It is required to grasp the statement, analyze the problem or policy it mentions and then decide which of the courses of action logically follow / most appropriate.Statement:The bike dealer found that there was a great response for the new ABC's bike booking with long queues of people complaining about the duration of business hours and arrangements.Courses of action:I) people should bring lunch and snacks while going for bike ABC's booking and be ready to spend several hours.II) Arrangement should be made for more booking desks and increase business hours to serve more people in less time.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.text: If either I or II follows
If neither I nor II follows
If both I and II follows

questions.text: In this section of logical deduction, the question consists of a brief passage (usually a report containing certain data regarding some social or economic problem), followed by certain inferences based on it. The student is required to analyze the contents (or data) of the passage and grasp the desirable facts from it. Then, student has to consider each of the given inferences in the context of the given passage. decide upon its degree of truth or falsity and then choose the hest alternative provided accordingly.In the overall economy of Pakistan, agriculture is the largest sector of economic activity. It plays a crucial role in the country's economic development by providing food to its people and raw materials to industry. It accounts for the largest share to the national income. The share of the various agricultural commodities, animal husbandry and ancillary activities has been more than 40 percent since independence. During the decade of the fifties, it actually contributed about half of the national output.Agriculture contributes to national income more than all other activities put together.
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: This option suggests that the statement is unquestionably accurate. However, the passage does not provide absolute confirmation; hence, it cannot be deemed 'definitely true.'

This option indicates that the statement is likely accurate based on the evidence presented in the passage. Given that agriculture plays a significant role in national income and economic development, this option is the most justifiable choice.

This option implies that there is insufficient information to make a decision. However, the passage provides enough data regarding agriculture's contribution to national income, making this option incorrect.
This option suggests that the statement is likely incorrect. Given the clear evidence in the passage supporting agriculture's contribution, this option is not valid.
This option asserts that the statement is unequivocally incorrect. The passage presents strong evidence of agriculture's significant role, rendering this option incorrect.

questions.explanation: The correct answer is 'Probably true' because the passage clearly states that agriculture is the largest sector of economic activity in Pakistan and contributes significantly to national income. The statement in question aligns with the information provided, suggesting a strong likelihood of its truth. The other options are incorrect because they either overstate the certainty of the statement (as in 'Definitely true') or underestimate the evidence provided (as in 'Data inadequate' and 'Probably false').

questions.text: Passage: Four players A, B C and D are holding 4 cards each. Each of them has an Ace, a King, a Queen and a Jack. All of them have all the suits (spades, hearts, clubs and diamonds). A has Ace of spades and Queen of diamonds. B has Ace of clubs and King of diamonds. C has Queen of clubs and King of spades. D has Jack of clubs. Jack of hearts is with:
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: A has the Ace of spades and Queen of diamonds. The only Jack not ruled out by other players' known cards is the Jack of hearts, which must be with A.
B possesses the Ace of clubs and King of diamonds. Since D has the Jack of clubs, and A must have the Jack of hearts, B cannot have the Jack of hearts.
C is holding the Queen of clubs and King of spades. With D holding the Jack of clubs, C cannot have the Jack of hearts.
D is in possession of the Jack of clubs. As each player can only have one Jack, D cannot have the Jack of hearts.

questions.explanation: By creating a table and filling in the known cards for each player, we can use a process of elimination to determine the remaining card distributions. This logical deduction reveals that player A must be holding the Jack of hearts.

questions.text: Directions: Read the following information and give answer.H, Q, C, L, N and F are six members of a family. There are two married couples among them. C is the mother of H and F.N is the father of L.H is the grandson of Q. The total number of female members in the family is three.How is Q related to F?
questions.subject: Logical Reasoning

options.explanation: Q cannot be the sister of F because Q is the mother of C. Since C is the mother of F, Q is the grandmother, not the sister.

Members: H, Q, C, L, N, FTwo married couplesC is the mother of H and F → C is femaleN is the father of L → N is maleH is the grandson of Q → Q is H’s grandparentTotal females = 3 → C + 2 othersSince H is the grandson of Q, Q must be grandparent, i.e., parent of either H’s mother or father.H’s parents: C (mother) + ??? (father not mentioned)There are two married couples, likely:C + ??? (H and F’s father)N + ??? (L’s mother)Females = 3: C + ??? + ??? → so Q is female (grandmother), the third female.C is mother of FQ is parent of C (since H is C’s son, H is Q’s grandson)Therefore, Q is F’s grandmother
Q is not the wife of F. The information clearly establishes that Q is the grandmother of F, not his wife.
The data is sufficient to determine that Q is the grandmother of F. The relationships provided are clear and complete.

questions.explanation: From the given information: C is the mother of H and F, and Q is the mother of C. These relationships establish that Q is the grandmother of both H and F. Therefore, Q is related to F as a grandmother, which confirms that Option B is correct. The other options are incorrect because they do not fit the established family tree: Q cannot be the sister or wife of F, and the data provided is sufficient to determine the relationship accurately.
